verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted but exists in a large latent complex (LLC) where the LAP prodomain remains disulfide-bonded to the mature dimer and the complex is covalently tethered to LTBP proteins, which in turn can be crosslinked to ECM or to cell-surface TM proteins (LRRC32/GARP, LRRC33/NRROS). On certain cell types (Tregs, platelets, macrophages), GARP or NRROS covalently anchors the latent TGF-β1 complex to the plasma membrane outer face in a wash-resistant manner. This constitutes stable surface attachment via a TM scaffold, making the protein body accessible from the extracellular face in those contexts.
